description:
Goal: make Compiler2 semantic convergence honest by modeling activation inputs as joined caller contributions inside a compiler-owned fixpoint engine, with a single compiler-owned type universe and exact within-run fact/change tracking. Signal: the quicksort trace-summary shows no double activation, no duplicate settled publications, no dead transient activations in the final closure path, no pre-drive runtime parsing, no fresh type universes in Compiler2 production code, and the docs describe activation input, semantic closure, and type ownership as value facts over compiler-owned ids rather than side stores or structural fingerprints.

Strategy: derive stable recursion and dispatch-key facts first, make fact slots value-aware, move activation input into multi-owner fact aggregation, collapse semantic closure into a read-only predicate, then finish the foundational type-world cleanup by making Compiler2 own the full interned type store locally, migrating all type-bearing facts/keys to that one world-owned store, and replacing structural type fingerprinting with exact id-backed values plus revisions before docs/backend work resumes.

This epic is driven by ../five-problems.html. The central move is that Activation(root, fn, key).input is not an imperative update in ActivationMap; it is the refine_widen join of every caller-owned contribution. The follow-on move is that Compiler2's types are not an imported substrate from the old world; they are one more compiler-owned identity domain, like code/modules/functions. Once those two moves are true together, the closure walk, duplicate activation, revision-counter fingerprinting, per-activate recursion peek, and throwaway type-universe calls stop being necessary machinery.
title: Compiler2 fixpoint: model activation inputs as fact joins
